title_en: The quiet balayage looks defining this season
title_el: The quiet balayage looks defining this season
excerpt_en: Soft, sun-kissed and grown-out by design. The shapes our regulars are asking for right now.
excerpt_el: Soft, sun-kissed and grown-out by design. The shapes our regulars are asking for right now.
body_en:
This season's balayage is quieter than the bold sun-streaks of a few years ago. Clients are asking for soft, lived-in dimension that grows out beautifully — colour you can put off touching for four months and still look intentional.

The technique itself is the same. The difference is restraint. Fewer, softer pieces, lower contrast, and root colour that meets the highlights mid-shaft rather than at the scalp.
body_el:
This season's balayage is quieter than the bold sun-streaks of a few years ago. Clients are asking for soft, lived-in dimension that grows out beautifully — colour you can put off touching for four months and still look intentional.

The technique itself is the same. The difference is restraint. Fewer, softer pieces, lower contrast, and root colour that meets the highlights mid-shaft rather than at the scalp.

title_en: How to maintain salon colour at home
title_el: How to maintain salon colour at home
excerpt_en: The right shampoo, the right water temperature, and the bottle we never travel without.
excerpt_el: The right shampoo, the right water temperature, and the bottle we never travel without.
body_en:
Three small changes will keep a £180 balayage looking fresh for months. Wash less often. Wash in cooler water. And use a colour-safe shampoo with a sulphate-light formula.

Anyone who tells you weekly Olaplex is optional has never tried a month without it. The bonds in colour-treated hair need rebuilding.
body_el:
Three small changes will keep a £180 balayage looking fresh for months. Wash less often. Wash in cooler water. And use a colour-safe shampoo with a sulphate-light formula.

Anyone who tells you weekly Olaplex is optional has never tried a month without it. The bonds in colour-treated hair need rebuilding.

title_en: Why every visit starts with a consultation
title_el: Why every visit starts with a consultation
excerpt_en: A few quiet minutes before any colour or cut. The single biggest reason guests come back.
excerpt_el: A few quiet minutes before any colour or cut. The single biggest reason guests come back.
body_en:
We start every visit with a consultation. Not a script, not an upsell — five minutes of real conversation about what you want, what your hair is doing, and what we think will actually suit you.

Half the time we end up doing something different from what was booked. Better to find out at the chair, with coffee in hand, than under the dryer.
body_el:
